summary: Matched `src/melee/gr/grtmewtwo.c` to 100.00% by replacing remaining inline assembly / NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs with C, refining `grTMewtwo_UnkStruct` from a single opaque field into eight `lb_UnkAnimStruct*` slots, and implementing the remaining small boolean callbacks plus the larger `grTMewtwo_802224B4` terrain-animation lookup helper.

key_files.role: Only changed file; converted remaining `grTMewtwo` ASM stubs to matching C and typed the local animation table struct.

what_changed: `grTMewtwo_UnkStruct` changed from one `UNK_T x0` field to eight typed `lb_UnkAnimStruct*` fields at offsets `x0`, `x4`, `xC`, `x8`, `x10`, `x14`, `x1C`, and `x18`.
Three 8-byte boolean stage callbacks, `grTMewtwo_80222398`, `grTMewtwo_802223F8`, and `grTMewtwo_80222488`, were converted from ASM wrappers returning `li r3, 0` into C functions returning `false`.
`grTMewtwo_802225C8` was converted from ASM returning `li r3, 1` into a C function returning `true`.

`grTMewtwo_802224B4` was converted from a 276-byte ASM function into C. It rejects `arg0 == -1`, calls `mpLib_80056B6C(arg0)`, rejects `-1`, then dispatches group `0` through `inlineA0` and group `1` through `inlineA1`, otherwise returning `NULL`.
Two static inline helpers, `inlineA0` and `inlineA1`, were introduced to map `mpLib_80054C6C(arg0)` bit flags `(1 << 0)` through `(1 << 3)` to entries in `grTMewtwo_804D6B08`.
`inlineA0` maps terrain flag values to the first four animation pointers: `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, and `xC`; `inlineA1` maps to the second four: `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, and `x1C`.
The PR report shows every listed function in `grtmewtwo.c` reached 100.00%, and the file score became `0 / 25,400`, indicating full match for this file.
smart_moves: Kept trivial return-only ASM functions as simple C `return false;` or `return true;`, avoiding unnecessary matching complexity.
Split the repeated `mpLib_80054C6C` bitflag-to-struct-field logic into two static inline helpers, which mirrored the original branch structure while keeping `grTMewtwo_802224B4` readable.

Used `(1 << n)` expressions for flag comparisons instead of raw constants `1`, `2`, `4`, and `8`, making the relationship to bit masks explicit without changing the generated logic according to the reported match.
Typed the previously opaque data table as `lb_UnkAnimStruct*` entries, enabling direct field access in the matched C implementation.
Preserved offset-style field names (`x0`, `x4`, etc.) for a partially understood struct, which improved type information while avoiding unsupported semantic names.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, many remaining callbacks may be exact constant-return functions; converting `li r3, 0/1; blr` ASM to `return false/true` can remove boilerplate cleanly.
When an ASM function repeats the same flag dispatch for two related cases, extracting duplicated switch/if logic into static inline helpers can be a viable matching tactic.
Opaque stage data structs can often be advanced incrementally by replacing `UNK_T` with typed pointer fields while retaining offset-based names until the semantic purpose is known.

The order of struct fields in source does not have to be semantically sorted; here fields are declared in the order needed to preserve offsets and access patterns, including `xC` before `x8` and `x1C` before `x18`.

Map/collision helper return values such as `mpLib_80056B6C` and `mpLib_80054C6C` can drive terrain-specific animation table selection; future ground-stage decomp should look for similar table patterns.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remained address-based with the `grTMewtwo_80xxxxxx` convention.
The local unknown struct retained the module-prefixed name `grTMewtwo_UnkStruct`.
Partially understood fields used offset-style names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, and `x1C`.
Temporary inline helpers were named generically as `inlineA0` and `inlineA1`, signaling a matching aid rather than confirmed game semantics.
Typed animation fields use `lb_UnkAnimStruct*`, preserving uncertainty about the actual animation data while improving on `UNK_T`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `ASM`/`#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)`/`NOT_IMPLEMENTED` blocks with direct C implementations after matching.
Matched 8-byte ASM callbacks by using direct boolean returns: `return false;` for functions that compiled to `li r3, 0; blr`, and `return true;` for `li r3, 1; blr`.
Implemented `grTMewtwo_802224B4` using nested conditionals rather than a switch, matching the original comparison-and-branch flow shown in the assembly excerpt.
Used `static inline` helper functions for repeated flag dispatch, likely to keep calls inlined and preserve the original function's layout.
Returned `NULL` for all invalid sentinel cases: input `-1`, `mpLib_80056B6C` returning `-1`, unhandled group values, and unrecognized bit flags.
The C code preserves the two-stage lookup: first classify with `mpLib_80056B6C`, then select an animation pointer using `mpLib_80054C6C`.
follow_up_queries: Find other `grT*` stage files with `mpLib_80056B6C` plus `mpLib_80054C6C` dispatch to compare animation table struct layouts.
Search for other remaining `ASM bool` functions that only return constant true or false and can be converted similarly.
Look for uses of `lb_UnkAnimStruct*` arrays or offset-named animation tables to refine `grTMewtwo_UnkStruct` semantics.
Compare `grTMewtwo_802224B4` against analogous functions in other stage modules to determine semantic names for `inlineA0`, `inlineA1`, and the struct fields.
